intended_use: Acorn 3D Software is a modular image processing software intended for use as an interface for visualization of medical images, segmentation, treatment planning, and production of an output file. The A
intended_use_full: Acorn 3D Software is a modular image processing software intended for use as an interface for visualization of medical images, segmentation, treatment planning, and production of an output file. The Acorn 3D Segmentation module is intended for use as a software interface and image segmentation system for the transfer of CT or CTA medical images to an output file. Acorn 3D Segmentation is also intended for measuring and treatment planning. The Acorn 3D Segmentation output can also be used for the fabrication of physical replicas of the output file using additive manufacturing methods, Acorn 3DP Models. The physical replica can be used for diagnostic purposes in the field of musculoskeletal and craniomaxillofacial applications. The Acorn 3D Alignment and Measurement module contains registration capabilities and measurement functionality based on anatomical reference geometry. It is intended to allow the user to align anatomical structures between datasets, perform spinopelvic measurements on 3D models of anatomy, and plan surgical procedures in pediatric and adult patients.
target_condition: Spinal deformities, degenerative spinal disease, lower limb alignment disorders, scoliosis, and conditions requiring surgical planning in pediatric and adult patients
ground_truth: For machine learning models: manually segmented CT images by highly-trained experts from in-house Mighty Oak Medical database and publicly-available VERSE '20 and CTPelvic1K datasets verified by multiple levels of expert review. For alignment accuracy: ground truth anatomical landmark positions used to calculate Target Registration Error.
other_metrics: Target Registration Error (TRE) for alignment: Slot Beam Median TRE 1.70 mm (3rd Quartile 2.74 mm); Cone Beam Median TRE 4.33 mm (3rd Quartile 5.58 mm). Machine learning model performance: Vertebral model Mean DICE Similarity Coefficient (DSC) 0.9331 on in-house testing (35 cases, 450 vertebral levels) and 0.94451 on VERSE '20 dataset (36 cases, 401 vertebral levels). Sacral model Mean DSC 0.96630 on in-house testing (40 cases).
